title: Israfels splitting
wikitext:
[]: Israfel's splitting             Quote Post by V » Fri Dec 14, 2007 1:08 pm Does Israfel voluntarily split apart when Asuka slices it in half, or what? in the final fight, does it simply voluntarily split again? Because if you rewatch the scene, there's...what appears to be one of those metals wall-shields that rise out of the ground (like the one they used to shield from its beam attack). --->I cannot tell if this wall-shield was basically right underneath Israfel's, er, crotch, and basically shot straight up and split the Angel in half....or if that was not a wall-shield rising from the ground at all, but just a building behind it. Further, Israfel doesn't BLEED at all when it splits so I wonder if this is actually it voluntarily splitting: I mean if we wanted to see a "dissected Angel" that's pretty much it. But it doesn't have internal organs or anything. I don't know if this means "Angels have no internal organs" or "Israfel's splitting in half by Asuka isn't representative of normal Angel insides because it does that voluntarily".

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Fri Dec 14, 2007 1:09 pm 2 cores = 2 souls. Israfel was 2 Angels merged into one and could split apart as needed.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Fri Dec 14, 2007 1:12 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: 2 cores = 2 souls. Israfel was 2 Angels merged into one and could split apart as needed.

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Fri Dec 14, 2007 1:17 pm What is an accurate example of the inside of a species that can alter it's form at will?

[]: Re: Israfel's splitting             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Dec 14, 2007 1:21 pm V wrote: Does Israfel voluntarily split apart when Asuka slices it in half, or what?

[]: Re: Israfel's splitting             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Fri Dec 14, 2007 1:25 pm Reichu wrote: Or was it one soul that could split between two bodies...?

[]: Re: Israfel's splitting             Quote Post by V » Fri Dec 14, 2007 1:26 pm Reichu wrote: The two Evas throw batons that generate some kind of magnetic field that induces Israfel to split in half, but I can't claim to know how it works.

[]: Re: Israfel's splitting             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Dec 14, 2007 1:30 pm V wrote: SAUCE?

[]: Re: Israfel's splitting             Quote Post by V » Fri Dec 14, 2007 1:46 pm Reichu wrote: V wrote: SAUCE?

[]: Re: Israfel's splitting             Quote Post by BobBQ » Fri Dec 14, 2007 3:48 pm Reichu wrote: Or was it one soul that could split between two bodies...?

[]:             Quote Post by jenova7 » Fri Dec 14, 2007 6:42 pm it would be more plausible if there were two souls The two Evas throw batons that generate some kind of magnetic field that induces Israfel to split in half, but I can't claim to know how it works.

[]: Re: Israfel's splitting             Quote Post by Sachi » Fri Dec 14, 2007 8:21 pm BobBQ wrote: Reichu wrote: Or was it one soul that could split between two bodies...?

[]:             Quote Post by jenova7 » Fri Dec 14, 2007 8:45 pm because the cores merge again as one just when it dies.

[]:             Quote Post by Ironfoot » Fri Dec 14, 2007 8:48 pm Looks like a yin-yang to me, blue=Shinji and red=Asuka. That was easy.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Dec 15, 2007 5:35 am jenova7 wrote: the lightings bad but that looks like 2 cores to me

[]:             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Sat Dec 15, 2007 6:23 am Hey Reichu, that link don't work. Well atleast there isn't a pic there...

[]:             Quote Post by jenova7 » Sat Dec 15, 2007 6:43 am i remember seeing both cores close together, but not combined on destruction. both were smashed separately by each eva's foot

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Dec 15, 2007 6:50 am Anonymous_Evafan wrote: Hey Reichu, that link don't work. Well atleast there isn't a pic there...

[]:             Quote Post by jenova7 » Sat Dec 15, 2007 6:56 am but...wouldn't it be easier to destroy one core if they really did recombine?

[]:             Quote Post by Semisubtle » Sun Dec 16, 2007 2:04 pm There are two cores visible on the Angel's body when together, and one each while split. Isn't that enough to prove the angel has two cores and therefore two souls?

[]: Israfel's splitting             Quote Post by V » Fri Dec 14, 2007 1:08 pm Does Israfel voluntarily split apart when Asuka slices it in half, or what? in the final fight, does it simply voluntarily split again? Because if you rewatch the scene, there's...what appears to be one of those metals wall-shields that rise out of the ground (like the one they used to shield from its beam attack). --->I cannot tell if this wall-shield was basically right underneath Israfel's, er, crotch, and basically shot straight up and split the Angel in half....or if that was not a wall-shield rising from the ground at all, but just a building behind it. Further, Israfel doesn't BLEED at all when it splits so I wonder if this is actually it voluntarily splitting: I mean if we wanted to see a "dissected Angel" that's pretty much it. But it doesn't have internal organs or anything. I don't know if this means "Angels have no internal organs" or "Israfel's splitting in half by Asuka isn't representative of normal Angel insides because it does that voluntarily".
